title: Second Battle of Onderon
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Onderon}} {{Battle |prev=[[Funeral of Amanoa|First Battle of Onderon]][[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] |conc= |next=[[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] |image=[[File:Tales_of_the_Jedi_-_The_Freedon_Nadd_Uprising_031.jpg]] |name=Second Battle of Onderon |conflict=[[Freedon Nadd Uprising]] |date=[[3998 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |place=[[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] |result=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] victory |side1=*Galactic Republic *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side2=*[[Naddist]]s[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] *[[Nomi Sunrider]] *[[Oron Kira]] *[[Alliance I Captain]] |commanders2=*[[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]]† *[[Novar (Human)|Novar]]† |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1= *[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] *[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] *~100 [[Republic battlecruiser]]s *[[Republic command ship]]s *[[Republic troop carrier|Rocket-jumper troopships]] *[[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|Republic rocket-jumpers]] *[[Republic Army|Republic troopers]] *[[Jedi Cruiser]]s *[[Beast Riders]] *[[Drexl|Beast Rider war-mounts]] *[[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] soldiers |forces2=*King Ommin *Novar *[[Naddist army]] **[[Naddist]] troops **[[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] **[[Darksider]] battalions **[[Sith Elite Warbot]]s *[[Assault shuttle/Legends|Dark side assault shuttles]] *[[Tank/Legends|Siege vehicles]] *[[Artillery/Legends|AA artillery]] *[[Artillery/Legends|AP artillery]] **[[Turbolaser/Legends|Turboblaster]] cannons |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Kith Kark† *Unidentified number of Republic troops *Unidentified number of Beast Riders |casual2=*King Ommin† *Novar† *Unidentified number of Naddists *Unidentified number of Darksiders |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|First, we must go to Coruscant, to speak with the senators of the Republic. I think you will need their help, for the enemies you faced today, are nothing compared to the dark-side power that awaits us on Onderon!|Thon, to Nomi Sunrider, concerning the troubles on Onderon|audio=Thon-Coruscant.flac|}} The '''Second Battle of Onderon''' occurred during the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3998 BBY]], resultant of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic]] [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] intervention solicited by the [[Tchuukthai]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. Along with his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Nomi Sunrider]], Thon attended the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] [[Ministry of Defense]] hearing called to discuss the growing [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] threat on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Sunrider was chosen to lead the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic fleet]] in battle against the worshipers of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]], who were united under the banner of Onderon's ruler, [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]]. Upon arrival in Onderonian [[Realspace/Legends|space]] under escort of the full [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] Navy, Nomi Sunrider and a team of four other [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] engaged Ommin and his [[Naddist]] [[Naddist army|army]]. The battle commenced just as [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] aristocrats [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] arrived on Onderon with the intentions of deciphering an ancient [[Sith spellbook/Legends|Sith spellbook]]. Caught in the midst of battle, the Ketos were shot down and crashed into [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], where, after searching for a time, were led to Ommin, who introduced them to [[Sith/Legends|Sith lore]]. Before he could begin their initiation into Sith teachings, they were ambushed by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights. The Jedi ultimately triumphed over the [[Sith sorcerer|sorcerer]] King, rescued the [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] Jedi Master [[Arca Jeth]], defeated Ommin's army, and freed Onderon of Nadd's dark influence. The Ketos also escaped Onderon, and, armed with their [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]], became the new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentices]] of Nadd. ==Background== ===Meeting of the Defense Committee=== {{Quote|Order, order!! This meeting of the Defense Committee will come to order! Thank you. Now, you're all aware of the trouble on the planet Onderon. But I'm afraid it's much worse than we suspected.|Minister of Defense Netus|audio=Netus.flac|}} In the year [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] and his three [[Padawan/Legends|apprentices]], as [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|watchmen]] of the [[Japrael system/Legends|Onderon system]], discovered that the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] held the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] under its sway. Over the course of the next two years, the Jedi revealed that the Onderonian royal family had been carrying on the legacy of the long-dead [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Freedon Nadd]]. As a result, the Knights had become embroiled in war against the rulers of Onderon and their [[Naddist]] followers. Realizing the threat to be more than a match for just four Jedi, Jeth requested assistance from the watchman of the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]], the [[Tchuukthai]] Master [[Thon]]. Thon gladly obliged, sending his student [[Oss Wilum]] to assist Jeth and his apprentices with the uprising on Onderon. Thon however, felt it was time to also seek the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] intervention. Traveling to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with his new protégé, [[Nomi Sunrider]], Thon attended an already-in-progress session of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Ministry]]. The [[Senator/Legends|senators]] had recently received a garbled distress message sent to Coruscant by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. In response, the senators engaged in a heated debate over whether to send aid to Onderon, or let the Jedi handle their own affairs. Thon and Sunrider entered the [[Grand Convocation Chamber]], where, through Sunrider's translating of his native [[Tchuukthese]], Thon addressed the Senate. [[File:DefenseMinistry.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Defense Ministry's meeting.]] Thon implored the assembly to come to the Jedi's aid, reminding them that the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]] had aided the Republic for generations, and that once the dark side subdued Onderon, it would sweep across the galaxy indiscriminately. The Jedi Master also had a surprise in store for his apprentice. Sunrider learned that Onderon was to be her first real mission as a Jedi, joining her fellow Knights in. Thon won the senators over with his rousing pleas, and Sunrider was chosen to command the full might of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Galactic Republic Navy]] against the followers of Freedon Nadd immediately. Around the same time, two young nobles from the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] had also arrived on Coruscant. [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]] and his cousin, [[Aleema Keto|Aleema]], the heirs to the [[Tetan monarchy/Legends|Tetan Monarchy]], visited Coruscant's [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]], in search of dark side knowledge. They stole a [[Sith spellbook/Legends|Sith spellbook]] from an exhibit and fled back to their lodging quarters, but quickly realized that they were unable to decipher the book's text. The Ketos' hope was renewed, however, when they heard a news report about [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] on Onderon, who practiced ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. They knew that Onderon was where the secrets of the book had any chance of being revealed to them, and immediately left for that planet. ===Trouble in Iziz=== {{Dialogue|Oron|Well we've got troubles of our own, Ulic. The darksiders have captured the palace. If we don't do something soon, the city is lost!|Ulic|Sent a distress message to the Republic. I don't know if it got through.|Cay|Well if they don't get here soon it won't matter if they get here at all!|attr=Ulic Qel-Droma, Cay Qel-Droma, and Oron Kira|audio=OronUlicCay.flac|src=}} Back on Onderon, the Jedi, and [[drexl]]-mounted [[Beast Riders]] of the planet's wilderness, were engaged in fierce combat with the Naddists in the capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. Arca Jeth, Ulic Qel-Droma, and Queen Galia, daughter of the Onderonian ruler, the [[Sith sorcerer]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]], located the King and his Dark Sider Army within the catacombs under the Royal Palace, and Master Jeth confronted him over the sarcophagus of Freedon Nadd. Ommin revealed that he had been in league with Nadd's ghost all along, even as the specter of the Dark Lord rose up behind him. Nadd then ordered the [[Sith trooper armor/Legends|armored]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Warb Null]] to engage Qel-Droma, but the [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] was only a ruse of the Dark Lord, and Null's [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]], Ommin. The real prize was Arca Jeth, whom Ommin attempted to capture while Qel-Droma and Null fought. [[File:2ndBattleOfOnderon.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Beleaguered Jedi and Beast Riders defend Fortress Kira against the Naddists.]] Newly empowered by the sudden arrival of Nadd, Ommin captured Jeth and carried him deeper into the catacombs of his lair, as Qel-Droma finished with Null, but could not save Jeth. Realizing that pursuing the King was a futile plan at best, Qel-Droma and Galia escaped the tunnels, closely followed by a contingent of her father's Dark Side Army. They emerged only to find battle fully underway aboveground, where they met up with Jedi [[Tott Doneeta]], who ferried Galia away to the ''[[Nebulon Ranger]]'' while Qel-Droma went to meet with his brother Cay and Galia's new husband, the [[Beast-Lord]] [[Oron Kira]]. Kira, the Beast Riders, and the remaining Jedi had been held up for two days fighting the Naddists. The enemy had retaken the [[Iziz Royal Palace|palace]], and Kira was preparing to retreat to his own fortress just as Ulic Qel-Droma arrived. Qel-Droma had sent a distress call to the Republic in hopes that they would send reinforcements, though he was unsure if it was received. Determined to save Jeth from Ommin's clutches, but surrounded by Naddists on all sides, the Jedi Knights and the armies of Oron Kira were left with only one option: to survive long enough for reinforcements to arrive. ==The battle== ===Republic relief=== {{Quote|All wings, this is Nomi Sunrider on board the Republic flagship ''Alliance 1''. We have achieved low orbit over Onderon. Status report!|Nomi Sunrider|audio=NomiAlliance1.flac|}} As Sunrider and the Republic Naval fleet entered Onderonian [[Realspace/Legends|space]], they began to stage their [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] attack run while preparing to deploy ground forces and a contingent of [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|air-to-ground troops]] as well. The [[General/Legends|generals]] were finalizing their battle plans when an unidentified civilian yacht recognized as being of [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] origin exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] right in the middle of the Republic Navy's bombardment. Despite warnings to steer clear of the security military zone, the civilian craft ignored the Navy's warnings and continued straight for the planet. As Republic rocket-jumpers began their air-to-ground assault over Iziz, the civilian vessel, carrying the Ketos, was caught in the crossfire, and crashed to the ground. The Ketos were able to escape the wreckage with their lives, and while searching through the city streets, managed to seek out authentic practitioners of the dark side. These [[Darksider]]s in turn brought them before Ommin. ===Jedi offensive=== {{Quote|Take cover! There's a dark side assault shuttle strafing the city!''"
"''No! Look! That ship's from the Republic! Reinforcements have arrived!|Cay and Ulic Qel-Droma|audio=CayUlic.flac|}} [[File:JediSeige.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Jedi reinforcements arrive to join the battle against the Naddists.]] Meanwhile, a team of Jedi Knights including [[Dace Diath]], [[Qrrrl Toq]], [[Shoaneb Culu]], and [[Kith Kark]] arrived on Onderon with Sunrider to assist Oss Wilum, Tott Doneeta, Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma, who themselves were trapped inside Kira's forest [[Kira Fortress|citadel]], taking heavy fire from the Naddist armies. Sunrider's Jedi team created a [[Force barrier/Legends|shield-wall]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as they fought towards their besieged comrades, and Sunrider used her [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to sway the tide of the conflict. Sunrider's meditation skills were proving most beneficial to the Jedi, and though their fighting spirit was restored as a result, Kark died when he was shot down by the Naddists, becoming one with the Force. The Jedi were also affected by the [[Waves of darkness|suppression of the dark side]] in the Onderonian [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], feeling their connections to the Force slowly being diminished. Sunrider managed to pinpoint the source to an old, incapacitated man, focusing his dark power somewhere nearby. The old man felt the novice Jedi woman's probe through the Force, and he lashed back at her with the dark side. Sunrider was knocked from her feet, just as Ulic Qel-Droma came up to her on the battlefield. It was their first meeting. Alarmed by the sight of a beautiful Jedi woman felled in battle, Qel-Droma rushed to her side. After conversing with her, Qel-Droma discovered that the old man whom she had felt attack her through the Force was indeed King Ommin. He told Sunrider that Ommin ruled Onderon under the power of Sith magic, and that with his powers he subdued Jeth. Gathering the rest of the Jedi Knights, Qel-Droma led them through the tunnels under the palace, into Ommin's lair. ===Confronting King Ommin=== {{Quote|How goes the battle in the city above us?''"
"''Not well, King Ommin. For the first two days, we drove the enemy back. But the Republic has arrived. And another Jedi, a female! They seemed to have evened the scales! Our Sith magic no longer controls ebb and flow!''"
"''All because of one new Jedi? Her power must be great! Hmm. I shall have to deal with her immediately.|King Ommin and Novar|audio=OmminNavar.flac|}} At the same time, the Ketos met with the crippled and seemingly weak Ommin, who was depending on a [[Ommin's mechanical exoskeleton|steel armature]] for physical support. Satal showed the old King their stolen Sith spellbook and asked Ommin to translate it for them. Ommin in turn gave him an ancient [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]], one of many Ommin had received from the last [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Freedon Nadd himself. With the amulet Satal was able to understand the ancient writings with ease. Ommin then took the book and gave it to his assistant [[Novar (Human)|Novar]] for duplicating. [[File:JediStrikeForce.jpg|thumb|right|290px|Jedi Knights ambush King Ommin in his lair.]] As Novar had the book transcribed, Ommin showed the Tetan heirs his "most prized possession," a Jedi Master, hanging on the wall and [[Torture/Legends|bound]] by the King's Sith magic. Aleema gasped in horror as Ommin explained his plan to crush Jeth under the power of the dark side and raise him back up as a Dark Jedi. Ommin then leaned close to Jeth, who was whispering the King that his time was up, and that his Jedi Knights had arrived. As if on cue, seven Jedi Knights rushed into Ommin's lair, destroying many [[Sith Elite Warbot]]s in the process. In the midst of the skirmish, Cay Qel-Droma was attacked from a blind spot and lost his droid arm. However, the Jedi pressed their way to Ommin and his Tetan visitors, who were completely surprised by the ambush. Ommin proved to be a most formidable and unexpected adversary as he blasted both Sunrider and Ulic Qel-Droma with waves of dark side energy. Before he could attack Qel-Droma again, the young Jedi slashed through the King's body with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Ommin sank to the ground like an [[Arkanian jellyfish]]. Before he could complete the transcription of the spellbook, Novar perished, having also been caught in the Jedi ambush. In the midst of all the commotion, Satal and Aleema Keto escaped into the tunnels. The Ketos retreated further into the depths under the castle, where they were met by the spirit of Freedon Nadd, who gave them a trove of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] that included their book as well as two [[Sith sword]]s that formerly belonged to Ommin. Nadd also told them that they were heirs to a great Sith destiny, and that wherever they went, Nadd would find them and begin their instruction in the ways of the dark side. He directed them to a passage leading to the surface, and the Ketos escaped the melee unscathed. Nadd then appeared by Ommin's side, whose [[Life support/Legends|life support]] contraption had been destroyed, rendering him utterly helpless. The Jedi had also successfully rescued Jeth, and upon seeing this, Nadd realized that the old King had failed him. Nadd withdrew his power from Ommin and reminded him that these Jedi did not belong to the dark side. Nadd soon corrected himself, however, stating that perhaps one or two of them might fall to darkness. Jeth denounced Nadd's spirit as Ommin passed into darkness himself, and congratulated Qel-Droma on his victory. Nadd only laughed at Jeth and told him that in reality the Jedi had lost that day, as he would soon realize. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Ulic and Nomi Sunrider seem to be enjoying one another, Master Arca!''"
"''Yes, Cay. Their laughter is welcome after all the grief this planet has borne.|Cay Qel-Droma and Master Arca, concerning the peace on Onderon|audio=CayArca.flac|[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]}} [[File:DrexlRidin.jpg|thumb|left|170px|The Jedi enjoy a brief moment of relaxation while riding drexls.]] As the Republic established martial law on Onderon, off-worlders were evacuated to their [[Homeworld/Legends|home planets]]. The customs personnel were told to stop anyone trying to get dark side weaponry off of Onderon, but Satal and Aleema Keto blended in with the masses and slipped past their check-points. The sarcophagi of King Ommin, his wife, [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]], and Freedon Nadd would later be transported to the far side of the [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]], as was originally planned before the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|Naddist Uprising]] began. Jeth had succeeded in cleansing Onderon, and the native people took to restoring civilization to their war-torn planet. With the threat of Ommin finally removed, Oron Kira and Galia could rule from the throne of Iziz as King and Queen of Onderon peacefully. The Jedi, after all their efforts, enjoyed a short season of respite on the planet, learning to ride Beast-Riders' mounts. But for Jeth, there was little room for relaxation. He had not forgotten about the two young Tetan nobles whose curiosity for dark side things led them to Ommin. Hearing news of a recent [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|political]] in the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] [[Star system/Legends|system]], the Jedi Master suspected that Sith magic had survived defeat on Onderon and made its way to the Tetan worlds. As a result Jeth accepted a Jedi watchman's responsibility for Empress Teta, and prepared his Jedi to face the dark side once again.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] ==Legacy== Freedon Nadd's prophecy, that one or two of the Jedi present would fall to the dark side, indeed came to pass. Of the eight surviving Jedi Knights, two of them became aligned with the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Ulic Qel-Droma would eventually defect and join the Ketos' Sith-inspired [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], and would also become a Dark Lord.''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]'' Oss Wilum would be seduced to the dark side by Dark Lord of the Sith [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], and would become possessed by a dark side spirit trapped within Kun's stolen [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]].''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The Second Battle of Onderon first appeared in the issue [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2: Initiates of the Sith'']] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] series of comics. The battle was conceived and written by author [[Tom Veitch]], and was drawn by artist [[Tony Akins]]. Originally, Veitch was contracted by [[Dark Horse Comics]] to do a "one-shot deal," and the end result was ''The Freedon Nadd Uprising''.[[Wookieepedia:Interview/Tom Veitch|Interview with Tom Veitch]] Eventually, both Veitch and [[Kevin J. Anderson]] would use the events portrayed in this battle and the Freedon Nadd Uprising as background details for their joint venture [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']], where such characters as Aleema and Satal Keto would be explored in depth while playing integral parts to the story line. The battle also provides a backdrop for the [[Krath Holy Crusade]], which was written as part of the larger [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. The battle was recounted in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]], written by [[John Whitman]] and based on the original script by Veitch. In the original comic appearance of the Second Battle of Onderon, Nomi Sunrider is on Ossus when she is chosen to lead a team of Jedi in assisting Arca Jeth's Knights. She then arrives on Onderon with a team of Jedi Knights including Dace Diath, Shoaneb Culu, Kith Kark, and Qrrrl Toq. In the audio version, however, Sunrider first accompanies Thon to Coruscant, completely bypassing Ossus. It is here that she is chosen to lead not the Jedi, but the Republic Fleet in the battle for Onderon. Also, the other Jedi Knights that arrived on Onderon in the comic with Sunrider were not mentioned in the audio drama. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] {{Mo}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Second Battle of Onderon}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *{{FactFile|115|REV 3-4|Naddist Revolt}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{1stID|Second Battle of Onderon}} *''[[Galaxy at War]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Zweite Schlacht von Onderon |pl=Druga bitwa o Onderon (3998 BBY) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onderon, Second Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of the Freedon Nadd Uprising]]
